regions.us-ports: Today, Charleston is experiencing light northerly winds at 5 knots under clear skies with no rain chance, providing excellent conditions for smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect overcast skies with northwest winds increasing to 8 knots and a 1% rain chance in Charleston, while nearby ports such as Savannah and Norfolk will see similar wind shifts and mostly dry conditions. By day three, winds will shift to a southerly direction at 10 knots with continued overcast skies and minimal rain chances, affecting Charleston and extending to Port Canaveral and Miami. These steady wind speeds and sky conditions should allow for routine port operations without disruption across the US Ports region. Passengers and crew should anticipate consistent overcast conditions but no significant weather impacts beyond light wind variations through May 5.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, a 46% rain chance with south winds at 9 knots and clearing skies may affect shore excursions and embarkation procedures in Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, SSE winds increasing to 15 knots combined with a 60% rain probability and overcast skies are expected to impact port operations in Key West, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios. By day three, east winds at 13 knots with a 54% chance of drizzle could affect disembarkation and tendering activities in Puerto Plata, La Romana, and Samaná. Passengers and crew should prepare for reduced visibility and wet conditions during these periods, especially at ports with the highest rain chances. We will continue monitoring wind shifts and rain probability trends closely for any operational advisories.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today in San Juan and across the Eastern Caribbean, we have clear skies with light southeast winds at 5 knots, ensuring smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations with no rain expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect easterly-southeast winds increasing to 11-13 knots with overcast skies but minimal rain probability below 3 percent, so port operations in San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua will continue without weather-related interruptions. Beyond 48 hours, easterly-southeast winds will steady near 11 knots with overcast conditions persisting and a slight increase in rain chance to 8 percent, which remains too low to affect cruise or shore activities. Overall, wind speeds and cloudy skies may slightly impact visibility but will not disrupt ship maneuvering or shore excursions at any of the scheduled ports.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, thunderstorms with a 14% rain probability are active near Cozumel with SSE winds at 6 knots, producing localized showers but no significant operational disruptions at Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, or Belize City at this time. However, thunderstorms with an 84% rain chance are expected today in Cozumel and surrounding Western Caribbean ports, likely impacting shore excursions and embarkation/disembarkation procedures due to heavier rain and SE winds at 7 knots. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, an elevated rain chance of 76% accompanied by drizzle with E winds at 5 knots will affect Cozumel and Belize City, resulting in slick conditions and possible delays in outdoor activities and tender operations. Beyond 48 hours, skies will remain overcast with a 33% rain probability and SE winds increasing to 9 knots, suggesting improving visibility and fewer weather-related impacts for all Western Caribbean ports. Passengers and crew should remain prepared for wet conditions today and tomorrow, especially in Cozumel and Belize City, while monitoring updates as conditions gradually improve on day three.
regions.southern-caribbean:
PRIORITY ALERT: Elevated east winds of 20 knots forecast for Day 3 will impact Aruba and Curacao.  

Today, Aruba sees east winds at 16 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain, allowing normal embarkation and shore excursions with minimal operational impact. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect east winds to increase to 18 to 19 knots with drizzle at Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, though rain chances remain below 10 percent and will not affect port operations. By Day 3, sustained east winds will reach 20 knots with drizzle continuing at Aruba and Curacao, requiring heightened attention to mooring and tendering due to elevated wind speeds. Beyond 48 hours, these steady east winds suggest continued elevated sea conditions that may lead to minor adjustments in shore excursion scheduling at Aruba and Curacao.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today in Barbados, we have clear skies with east winds at 10 knots and a 6% chance of rain, so port operations and shore excursions are running smoothly without weather concerns. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect east winds around 12 to 13 knots with light drizzle and rain probabilities between 12% and 16%, which do not pose a threat to cruise operations in Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada. Beyond 48 hours, the sustained east winds near 13 knots will continue alongside drizzle and a low 16% rain chance around Barbados, so cruise schedules and port activities in the Lesser Antilles should remain stable with no weather-related interruptions. Passengers and crew should monitor conditions but anticipate no significant weather impacts through this period.
regions.los-angeles: Today, winds at Los Angeles and San Pedro are WSW at 4 knots with overcast skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations across all area ports including Long Beach and Marina del Rey. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to increase from the WSW up to 12 knots along with overcast conditions and a slight rise in rain probability to 7 percent, which will coincide with light drizzle primarily impacting Catalina Island and the San Pedro port area, though no operational disruptions are anticipated. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to the west at 13 knots with continued drizzle and a rain chance increasing to 9 percent, maintaining overcast skies that may cause minor visibility reductions for vessels docking at Long Beach and Marina del Rey. No marine wind warnings are in effect at any port through this period, allowing for routine cruise ship maneuvering and shore excursions. Passengers and crew should remain aware of the light drizzle offshore but can expect generally stable conditions for port operations throughout the forecast window.
regions.ensenada: Today in Ensenada, winds are light from the west at 1 knot under overcast skies with no rain expected, allowing normal embarkation and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, west winds will increase to 8 knots with drizzle and a 5% rain chance on Day 1, then 11 knots with overcast skies and an 11% rain chance on Day 2, conditions that will not impact port operations at any of the three ports. By Day 3, however, west winds strengthen to 14 knots with a 58% probability of drizzle, which may affect shore excursions and disembarkation activities particularly at Ensenada and Punta Banda. Passengers and crew should prepare for reduced visibility and slick surfaces during Day 3’s drizzle. Beyond 48 hours, expect continued overcast skies and sustained west winds near 14 knots, maintaining the elevated chance of wet conditions in the region.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today in the Cabo San Lucas region, winds are light from the northwest at 6 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz to proceed without weather-related interruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing WNW winds at 15 knots on day one and 13 knots on day two, with skies transitioning from partly cloudy to overcast; these conditions will be consistent across all three ports, but remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, so embarkation and disembarkation should remain smooth. Beyond 48 hours, winds will ease to 9 knots from the WNW with continued overcast skies and no rain expected, maintaining steady but muted marine conditions for cruise operations through La Paz, Cabo San Lucas, and San Jose del Cabo. No marine wind warnings or precipitation impacts are forecast, supporting reliable scheduling for all cruise vessel movements and shore activities during this period.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the north at 1 knot with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, allowing smooth embarkation and disembarkation at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata with no weather-related delays for shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing westerly winds at 8 knots accompanied by overcast skies but still 0% rain probability, which will affect all three ports with potentially reduced visibility during tender operations but no interruption to scheduled activities. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to northwest at 9 knots with continued overcast conditions and no rain expected, maintaining stable but somewhat dim lighting conditions for cruise ship maneuvering and passenger transfers at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. No marine wind warnings are in effect, so all port and ship operations should proceed normally under these light to moderate wind conditions.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today, winds are light from the northeast at 2 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southwest to west-southwest winds increasing to 10 to 11 knots with persistent overcast skies and no rain, which should maintain smooth port operations across all four ports. These steady winds and cloud cover may slightly reduce visibility but will not impact vessel maneuvering or tendering activities. Beyond 48 hours, westerly winds near 10 knots and continued overcast conditions will prevail, keeping stable marine conditions without rain concerns for cruise operations throughout the region. Passengers and crew should anticipate consistent, moderate winds with no weather-related disruptions through the upcoming days.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, an east wind at 8 knots is bringing overcast skies with a 48% chance of rain showers that may affect shore excursions and embarkation procedures in Barcelona, Palma de Mallorca, and Valencia. In the next 24 to 48 hours, southwest winds increasing to 11 knots with an 18% rain probability will bring lighter rain showers, mainly impacting Valencia and Ibiza without significant operational disruptions. Beyond 48 hours, east winds at 7 knots will accompany a 58% chance of thunderstorms expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Passengers and crew should prepare for possible delays or changes in outdoor activities in these ports as the thunderstorm threat develops. Visibility and sea state remain moderate, but careful monitoring of conditions is advised, especially for day three.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, conditions in Naples show light northerly winds at 1 knot with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions across all Central Mediterranean ports including Marseille, Nice, and Genoa. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to westerly at 9 knots with partly cloudy skies on Day 1, followed by southerly-southwesterly winds at 6 knots and overcast skies on Day 2, maintaining minimal rain probabilities under 5% for Naples and nearby ports like Civitavecchia and Livorno. Looking ahead to Day 3, a 41% rain chance with southerly winds at 5 knots may affect shore excursions and port operations in Naples, Sardinia, Corsica, and potentially Dubrovnik, so crew and passengers should prepare for intermittent rain. No Small Craft Advisories or stronger wind warnings are forecast during this period, allowing for generally stable navigation and port activity.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, an overcast sky with a 58% rain chance and NNW winds at 10 knots will likely affect port operations and shore excursions in Athens (Piraeus) and nearby Eastern Mediterranean ports including Santorini and Mykonos. These conditions may cause delays in embarkation and disembarkation procedures, so passengers should plan accordingly for possible wet conditions and reduced visibility. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, NNW winds will ease to 6 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% rain chance, improving conditions in Athens and extending to Rhodes and Izmir, allowing smoother port activities. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to SSW at 8 knots with overcast skies returning, but no rain is expected, signaling stable conditions for Cyprus, Haifa, Alexandria, and Istanbul. Crew and passengers should remain alert for overcast skies today and adjust shore excursions in Athens, Santorini, and Mykonos accordingly.
